response: The 2007 documentary "Zoo" was more successful than the 1998 documentary "Divine Trash" based on its recognition at the Sundance Film Festival, where it won one of 16 awards out of 856 candidates. "Zoo" was selected as one of the top five American films to be presented at the Directors' Fortnight sidebar at the 2007 Cannes Film Festival. In contrast, "Divine Trash" is a 1998 documentary film directed by Steve Yeager about the life and work of John Waters, but there is no specific information provided about its success or ranking relative to "Zoo." Therefore, based on the provided context, "Zoo" was more successful than "Divine Trash."
